Huge bushy apple tree in the corner of S Fish Hatch and Brendan Ave, next to strip mall driveway. 

Apples are crisp and sweet; likely golden delicious. Tree is across Brendan (on Pizza Hut side) from where GPS marker dropped. Access: Permitted by owner.
Falling Fruit access: Permitted by owner.
2931 S Fish Hatchery Rd, Fitchburg, WI 53711, USA
Apples with good taste, but not very cosmetic. They make great apple butter and sauce!

Aldo Leopold Park. 6-8 black walnuts- no-mow area behind baseball backstop, on both sides of mowed path. crabapple- no-mow area to right of soccer field (facing street). Crabapple- city says to right of basketball--couldn't find, but did find a crabapple in th
Location is on the east side of Longford Terrace above and on the north side of the bike path tunnel. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-unknown.
3072 Yarmouth Greenway Dr, Fitchburg, WI 53711, USA

Elderberry bushes along the Capital City Trail and around the pond. Bring a good size bucket and scissors to minimize the number of berries that fall to the ground while harvesting! Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-unknown.
Taste like concord-- small fruit with large seed. On bike path. Access: Public.
Two trees near each other. 1.5" diameter purple and delicious fruit. On bike path. Access: Public.
Black raspberries off Nakoma Rd. I haven't harvested them, but noticed them just before they got rip this year. In the walking area around the Duck Pond.
There is one leggy, productive serviceberry 'bush' along bike path next to a ledge that one can stand upon to pick the berries. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-July.
Large apple tree west of restrooms, on the edge of the water. Unknown cultivar. Medium sized, sweet, crunchy apples. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Allied Drive near Red Arrow Trail - in front of a payday loan place - 5 golden apple trees - large and tasty
2 large apple trees in the median. Red apples. Access: Public.
Orchard Ridge Park. 5309 Loruth Terrace. Crabapple - we counted more than 20 crabapples & found 1 mulberry to right behind swings & 2 mulberries to left behind grey house with grapevine

Prolific apple tree with reddish-green fruit right to the left of the wooden Greentree neighborhood sign. At the head of the footpath, so I *think* this is city/public land. Easy to pick. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-October.
Black Walnut tree located on the east side of the bike path. Fruit falls on the path bust most can be found in the grass/brush to the east. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

Black Walnut tree on the west side of the path. Fruit mostly falls on the path but can also be found further down hill as they roll after falling off the tree. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Newer tree (2016) at only 2" diam planted on boulevard. Should be ready to fruit in a couple of years. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Orchard Ridge Park. 5309 Loruth Terrace. Crabapple - we counted more than 20 crabapples & found 1 mulberry to right behind swings & 2 mulberries to left behind grey house with grapevine

Just outside the door (to the left) of Barriques. Fruit is usually best late June to mid-July.

It's on private property, but I've just asked the baristas or manager if I can pick, and they're okay with it. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: June-July.

Small purple plums found on the forest side of the trail. Best around mid to late August, a mixture of sweet and tart. Unsure of the varietal but certain they are plums. You do not have to leave the trail to pick them. Access: Public.
